title: Provenance store + "no source, no claim" enforcement (content-addressed; license + verify status)
context: One of ATLTUAE's four refusals is 'no unsourced claim': every surfaced claim must carry verifiable, license-clean provenance. This safety-critical M0 subsystem implements the provenance store so claims are content-addressed, re-derivable, and tamper-evident, and so the synthesis layer physically cannot surface a claim that lacks provenance. A bypass here would break a refusal, so it is high risk-tier and gate-reviewed.
objective: Implement the provenance store and 'no source, no claim' enforcement: every claim links to one or more content-addressed Sources (identifier/URL, title, license + verified reuse status, retrieval date, content hash, derivation/query trail) and a verification status, with a citation-coverage test that fails the build on any unsourced surfaced claim.
acceptanceCriteria: Every claim links to >= 1 Source (identifier/URL, title, license + verified reuse status, retrieval date, content hash, derivation/query trail) and a verification status
The synthesis layer cannot surface a claim lacking provenance; a citation-coverage test fails the build if any surfaced claim is unsourced
Sources and claims are content-addressed so a claim is re-derivable (reproducibility foundation); the provenance chain is tamper-evident
Reviewed and signed off by the gate reviewer (recorded)
